units.title: Chitonga: ZYAMASIMPE

units.title: Cinyanja: Zocita-Exercise
topics: Phunziloli linena za Litilica. Litilica idziwika mosiyana-siyana kulingana ndi alembi
osiyana-siyana. Ngakhale lidziwika kuti Litilica, imafalitsidwa mnjila zosiyana-
siyana kupyolela m’nyimbo, masewelo, ndakatulo, miyambi, mikuluwiko ndi
zilapi (ndagi). Izi maka-maka zigwela m’mbali ziwili zimene zili mu zolembedwa

units.title: Cinyanja: Sankha ziganizo ziwili cabe mwa zotsatilazi, zimene zimasulila Litilica:
topics: a) Litilica ndi zimene zimafalitsidwa kapena kuphunzitsidwa
b) Litilica ndi zonse zooneka ndi maso.
c) Litilica ndi zija zimene zili zolembedwa m’mabuku,

units.title: Cinyanja: Peleka tanthauzo lako-lako la Litilica.
topics: Pali mitundu ya Litilica iwili, Litilica ya Zapakamwa ndi Litilica ya Zolembedwa.
Litilica ya Zapakamwa ifalitsidwa kupyolela m’kulankhula monga zilapi,
masewelo, ndakatulo ndi zina. Litilica ya Zolembedwa ipezeka m’mabuku, zozile

units.title: Cinyanja: Sankha zitsanzo ziwili zimene zinena za Litilica ya Zapakamwa mwa izi:
Cinyanja: Nena kusiyana pakati pa Litilica ya Zapakamwa ndi Litilica ya
Cinyanja: Nena kufanana pakati pa Litilica ya Zapakamwa ndi Litilica ya
Cinyanja: Fotokoza ubwino wa Litilica ya Zolembedwa.

suggestedActivities: Akenye bana ba sikolo mwa ngambolo ya kukona kufa taluso ya
zeshangelwa kwa masina ni musebezi wa teñi.
 Kushangela manzwi ki mukwa wa kuekeza zeshangelwa kwa masina a
 Musebezi wa kushangela ki kubupa manzwi amanca, kucinca taluso
Bakene mwa ngambolo ya kukona kufa taluso ya kushangela manzwi ni
assessmentMethods: Ka nako yakuituta kapa hasamulaho wa kuituta, bana ba sikolo baswanela
a) Kuba mwa ngambolo ya kubuisana.
b) Kubatisisa ni kufumana likalabo za kuitusisa milyani ka mafosisa.
c) Kuamuhela milao yeswanela kulatelelwa hakubuisanwa.
d) Kubeleka ni muluti kubupa sikuka sa kuitusisa kubata likalabo.
e) Kuambola ni muluti kuama mayemo amande akubuisana.
resources:  Samples of descriptive composition of people.
 Pictures of different people.
EXPECTED STANDARD: Description of a person written correctly. (This must come from
the syllabus as it is)
